archetypes.title: The Persona
archetypes.subtitle: The face you wear, the face you forget you're wearing.

primary_image.title: A Lady with a Squirrel and a Starling (Anne Lovell?)
primary_image.note: Holbein's lady is composed, finished, sittable. The squirrel and starling are accessories — small private creatures kept close to a public face.

archetypes.title: The Shadow
archetypes.subtitle: What you have refused to know about yourself.

primary_image.title: David with the Head of Goliath
primary_image.note: Caravaggio painted himself as the severed head of Goliath. The canonical Shadow image.

archetypes.title: The Anima
archetypes.subtitle: The interior figure of the gender you are not.

primary_image.title: The Lady of Shalott
primary_image.note: Waterhouse's Anima — the interior feminine, leaving the tower into the river of her own becoming.

archetypes.title: The Self
archetypes.subtitle: The whole of which the conscious self is a small part.

primary_image.title: Liber Divinorum Operum, Universal Man
primary_image.note: Hildegard's vision of the figure of humanity contained within the cosmos. Pre-figures everything Jung later named about the Self.

archetypes.title: The Wise Old Man
archetypes.subtitle: The interior guide. Not always old. Not always a man.

primary_image.title: Aristotle with a Bust of Homer
primary_image.institution: The Metropolitan Museum of Art, New York (Open Access)
primary_image.note: The living thinker meets the dead poet through touch. One Wise Old Man recognizing another.

archetypes.title: The Trickster
archetypes.subtitle: The boundary-crosser. The destroyer who clears the way.

primary_image.title: The Garden of Earthly Delights (full triptych)
primary_image.note: Eden on the left, delight in the middle, the consequences on the right. The Trickster is the whole triptych — what gets crossed, what gets crossed again.

mandalas.title: De integra microcosmi harmonia (from Utriusque Cosmi Historia)
